text:
There once was a house just beyond the sun's golden rays that had a secret garden. Everyone who knew about the garden loved to stop by. The flowers were a riot of spring colors, and in the shade beneath the trees, blooms that smelled like vanilla wafeds never really faded.

One spring morning, a curious kitten named Sylvester discovered the garden on a whim. He leaped higher each time he visited, curious about where this delightful secret spot could lead.

One of his explorations led him to an old, gnarled trunk. With cat determination, Sylvester poked it. A cool, dampness greeted him. His whiskers twitched, drawn in by something even more fascinating.

He dug deeper and tumbled headfirst into a burrow lined with soft moss. There, hidden under a cozy patch of moss, lay a treasure chest. Inside was a peculiar box. Carefully picking it up, Sylvester discovered it was made of what felt like glass but could bend
